def: adj. & s.m. Captivating the mind or heart, &c. (i.q. man-hārī, q.v.s.v. man); — s.f. (for S. mano-hāri), What captivates the mind, or charms, fascinates or delights, &c.
more: s. f. Charming, fascinating, delighting, captivating the mind.

def: s.m. (dialec.) = mānukh = mānush, q.v. s.m. A man; — a husband

ety: manu+ja
def: s.m. See s.v. منو manu. s.m. See منو manu.
s.m. = manush = manushya, qq.v. s.m. A man; — a husband
more: s. m. A man, man, mankind.

def: s. m. See مانس mānus.
s.m. corr. of mānush, q.v. s.m. A man; — a husband

ety: Prk. मणूसो; S. मनुष्यः
def: s.m. (dialec.) = manushya and mānus, qq.v. s.m. The being a man, the condition or the quality of a human being; human nature, humanity; manliness; man's estate, a man.
more: adj. انس affection. Attached; familiar. Aṅgrezoh se Hindustānī ab tak mānūs nahīṅ hue .
(part. pass. of انس) 1. adj. Associated, familiar, intimate. 2. s. m. f. A companion, associate, friend.
wety: Ultimately from Arabic مَأْنُوس (maʔnūs).

ety: Prk. माणुसो = S. मानुषः
def: s.m. A human being, a man; — husband.
more: s. m. A human being, a man. मांस māṅs, s. m. Flesh, meat.
wety: From Sanskrit मनुष्य (manuṣya), likely from a derivative of Proto-Indo-European *mon- (“man”).

ety: pass. part. of أنس 'to be sociable, or familiar,' &c.
def: part. adj. Associated; attached, friendly, familiar, intimate; — cheering, solacing, gladdening (to, -kā); — s.m. A companion, associate, friend, intimate, familiar.

ety: Prk. मणूसअइआ; S. मनुष्यता+इका
def: s.f. The state or condition of man; manhood, manliness, humanity; — (dialec.) any object of human pursuit (i.q. purushārth, q.v.)
subs: (bhal = bhalā, q.v.), s.f. Humanity; goodness; urbanity; honourableness, gentlemanliness.

ety: S. मनुष्यः, q.v.; Vedic मनुषः
def: s.m. A man; — a husband
more: n. m. S. मनुष्य Pr. mānuso . 1. A man ( purush , 1). 2. Husband. manush deh dhāran k. , v. n. To be incarnated as man. Hit an-hit pashu pakshi hu jānā, Mānuk tan, gun, gyān nidhānā . Ramāyan. Friendship and hate e'en birds and beasts do show, Knowledge and arts confined to man below.
s. m. Man. See منکهہ manukh.

ety: fr. manus
def: adj. & s.m. Belonging to mankind; appertaining to men; human; — a human being, a man; a person, an individual (vulg. mānus or mānas, q.v.)
s.f. A woman.
s.f. A woman; a wife.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian منشی (munšī), from Arabic مُنْشِئ (munšiʔ). Cognate with Bengali মুনশি (munśi).

def: s.m. The being a man, the condition or the quality of a human being; human nature, humanity; manliness; man's estate, a man.
adj. Human; — s.m. 'An offspring of Manu,' a human being, a man, mortal, being, person, individual; mankind (syn. ādamī, vulg. ādmī)
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Man-slaying, homicidal; one who slays a human being, a homicide, a murderer
s.m. The human form; — human existence
s.m. The human body, the human form; mortal flesh; — manushya-tan pānā or dharnā, To get, or to assume, a human body, to become incarnate
s.f. Traffic in human beings; slave-trade
part. adj. (f. -ā), Formed or made by man
s.m. Mankind, the human race; men, people.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit मनुष्य (manuṣyà). Doublet of مَنُکھ (manukh).

def: s.f. = S منشيتو मनुष्यत्व manushyatva, s.m. (fr. manushya, q.v.), The state or condition of man, manhood, humanity; manliness.
s.m. = S منشيتا मनुष्यता manushyatā, s.f. (fr. manushya, q.v.), The state or condition of man, manhood, humanity; manliness.
adj. Thinking, conceiving; minding, regarding.

ety: caus. of manānā, or doub. caus. of mannā
def: v.t. To cause to conciliate, or to be conciliated, &c. (see manānā).
s.m. See s.v. manu. s.m. See منو manu.
adj. (f. -ā), To be honoured or respected; worthy of honour; honourable, venerable, estimable, respectable; to be believed or credited, credible
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. -matī), Honoured, venerated, respected, esteemed; — an estimable person, &c.; — mānyā, s.f. An estimable woman; a highly respected woman.

def: s.f. = S مانيتو मान्यत्व mānyatva, s.m. (fr. mānya), Honourableness, venerableness, estimableness, worthiness, respectability; honour; credibility.
s.m. = S مانيتا मान्यता mānyatā, s.f. (fr. mānya), Honourableness, venerableness, estimableness, worthiness, respectability; honour; credibility.

ety: n. of place fr. نظر 'to see, behold,' &c.
def: s.m. Countenance, face, aspect, visage, looks; — an object of sight, a sight, a view; a landscape; a show, spectacle, theatre, scene
more: n. m. نظر saw. A sight; spectacle; view; landscape. manzar-i-ām , G. G. A conspicuous place.
(noun of place from نظر Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. 1. Countenance, face, visage, aspect. 2. A sight, spectacle, theatre.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَنْظَر (manzar), from Arabic مَنْظَر (manẓar).

ety: n. of place fr. نزل 'to alight, to descend,' &c.
def: s.f. A place for alighting, a place for the accommodation of travellers, a caravansary, an inn, a hotel; a house, lodging, dwelling, mansion, habitation, station; a mansion of the moon; story or floor (of a house); deck (of a ship); — a day's journey; — a stage (in travelling, or in the divine life); — place of destination, goal; boundary, end, limit
more: n. f. نزل descended. 1. A stage ( paṛāv , 1). Hasrat pe us musāfir-i-be-kas kī roiye Jo hak gayā ho pausihch ke manzil ke sāmne! Sarūr. Weep for the helpless traveller who lays him down and sighs, Footsore and weary on the road, the goal before his eyes. 2. A day's journey. 3. ( Ast .) A mansion of the moon ( nichhattar ). 4. A story or floor of a house. 5. A number (of boats or carts). manzil ba-manzil jānā ; H. paṛāo paṛāo jānā , v. n. To travel by stages. manzil pahuṅchānā , v. a. 1. To prove; bring home to one. 2. To perform one's obsequies. manzil denā , Mah.; bisrām d . Hin. v. a. To lay down a corpse for a while on the way to its resting place. manzil k., yā kāṭnā , v. a. To go one stage. manzil mārnā , v. n. To finish a difficult task. manzil-i-maqsūd , n. f. A goal; destination. manzil-i-maqsūd ko pahunchnā , v. n. 1. To reach one's destination. 2. To gain one's object. manzil h…

(noun of place of نزل Heb. and Aram. ) s. f. 1. A dayʼs journey, a stage. منزل کاٿنا manzil kāṭnā (dakh.) To finish a journey. 2. An inn, dwelling, station, mansion, story or floor of a house, deck, a mansion of the moon. ¶ munzil, part. act. (IV.) 1. Causing to alight, receiving hospitably. 2. Emittens semen. ¶ munzal, part. pass. Sent down, caused to alight or to descend.
subs: adv. From stage to stage; by stages; — at every atage
v.t. To convey (one) to his destination; to convey (one) to his last resting-place, to perform the obsequies of; — to bring (a thing) home to (a person), to prove to
(-ko), To lay down (a corpse) for a while on the way to its resting-place
or manzil karnā, To finish a journey; to get over a stage; to go one stage
v.n. To finish a difficult task
s.f. The goal of desire; goal, destination
To reach (one's) destination; — to gain (one's) object
A stiff journey or stage; a long stage or march.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَنزِل (manzil), from Arabic مَنْزِل (manzil, “house, dwelling”). First attested in c. 1421 as Old Hindi مَنْزِل (mnzl /⁠manzil⁠/, “abode”).

ety: for A. منزلة manzilat, fr. manzil
def: s.m. A story (of a house, — used in comp. to form adj., e.g. do-manzila, 'two-storied')
more: n. m. 1. Station; dignity (پايه 3). 2. ( In comp .) A story (of a house). ba-manzilah , adv. In the place of; instead of; as.
subs: In the degree or position (of); in the place (of), instead (of), as.

ety: for A. منزلة manzilat, fr. manzil
def: s.m. A story (of a house, — used in comp. to form adj., e.g. do-manzila, 'two-storied')
more: s. f. Dignity, station, condition, rank, post of honour.
subs: In the degree or position (of); in the place (of), instead (of), as.

ety: pass. part. of نظم 'to arrange in order,' &c.
def: part. adj. Arranged in order; joined; threaded, strung; — metrical, versified, in verse; poetic
more: (part. pass. of نظم Heb. ) adj. 1. Joined, threaded, arranged in order. 2. Metrical, versified, poetick.
subs: s.m. Anything placed in a series, row, line, or order; — a composition in rhyme or verse; verses; — rhyme.

ety: for A. منظومة manz̤ūmat, fem. of manz̤ūm
def: part. adj. Arranged in order; joined; threaded, strung; — metrical, versified, in verse; poetic
subs: s.m. Anything placed in a series, row, line, or order; — a composition in rhyme or verse; verses; — rhyme.

ety: pass. part. of نظر 'to see, look at,' &c.
def: part. adj. Seen, looked at; visible; admired; — chosen; approved of, admitted, accepted; sanctioned, granted; — agreeable; acceptable; admissible; — designed, intended: manz̤ūr-e-ḵẖudā, Approved of God; — God's will
more: adj. 1. Sanctioned; granted. 2. ( manzūr-i-nazar ) Approved; acceptable. manzūr-i-khudā , God's will. manzūr k. , v. a. See qubūl k . and mānnā (4, 6, 7). 1. To admit. 2. To agree to. 3. To confirm; affirm. manzūr-i-nazar , n. m. 1. A favorite; mistress. 2. A protege; minion. peshgāh se manzūr kīyā gayā , G. G. Received the assent of.

(part. pass. of نظر Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Seen, looked at, admired, visible, chosen, approved of, agreeable, acceptable, admissible, designed, intended; the object. منظورنظر manz̤ūr-i-naz̤ar, Chosen, agreeable to the sight, beloved; (subst.) A mistress, favourite.
subs: v.t. To approve, admit, accept; — to agree to; to sanction, to grant; — to confirm; to affirm

adj. Agreeable to the sight (of); chosen; approved; beloved; — s.m. An object of regard or affection, a beloved object; a sweetheart, a mistress; a favourite; a protégé; — pesh-gāh-se manz̤ūr kiyā gayā, Received the assent or sanction of the ruler or master, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian منظور (manzūr), from Arabic مَنْظُور (manẓūr).

ety: fr. manz̤ūr
def: s.f. Approval; choice; — admission; consent, permission, sanction; — admissibility
more: n. f. 1. Consent; permission; sanction. 2. Approval; choice ( qubūl ). manzūrī bā-qarīnah, yā zimnan , Tacit or implied consent. manzūrī-i-bil . Audit of a bill. manzūrī sānī . Re-admission. manzūrī khās , Special sanction. manzūrī kā muntazir rahnā , v. n. To wait sanction. manzūrī kī āsāmī , G. G. A Government appointment conveying a retiring pension. manzūrī nīlām , The confirmation of a sale. ba-manzūrī , adv. With the consent, approval, or sanction of. ba-shart-i-manzūrī , G. G. Subject to the approval of.
subs: or manz̤ūrī bā-ẓimnan, Tacit or implied consent
To be expecting, or to await, sanction
A Government appointment conveying a retiring pension
adv. With the approval, or consent, or sanction (of, -ě)
On condition of approval, &c.; subject to the approval (of, -ě).
wety: مَنْظُور (manzūr) + ـی (-ī).

ety: Prk. माउडिआ; S. मातृ+ल+इका
def: s.f. (dialec.) Mother, &c. ( = mā, or māʼī).
more: s. f. (dakh.) Mother: see مان māṅ.

def: s.m. (f. -ī) = موسا mausā, q.v. s.m. Mother's sister's husband, uncle.
Brij. n. f. Aunt ( khālā ).

ety: fr. māpnā, q.v.
def: s.m. Measurement; mensuration; — a system of measures and weights
more: n. f. S. मा to measure. 1. Measurement; survey. 2. A standard of measure or capacity. Bhar de abke to pūrā hī māp, mere hal-patī rājā! Rus. A cupfull grant, O king of ploughs! māp tol ; A. masāhat , n. f. Mensuration. māp kā pūrā . Full or standard height.
subs: adj. Of full or standard measure, or height, or size.

ety: perf. part. of māpnā, q.v.
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Measured; surveyed
subs: 'Measured soup with counted lumps (of meat)'; scarcity of provision; — penurious economy.

def: adj. & s.m. Serving for measuring or weighing; determining by measure, &c.; — a measurer; a surveyor; — (in Geom.) a gnomon.
more: n. m. 1. A measure. 2. A surveyor.

def: s.m. Measuring; — a pair of scales, a balance.

ety: i.q. mapānā, q.v.
def: s.m. Measuring; measure; quantity.

ety: caus. of māpnā, q.v., and = māp˚+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To cause to measure.
more: (causal of ماپنا) v. a. To cause to be measured.

ety: map(nā) + at = Prk. अत्ती = S. आप्तिः (āpi, caus. augment+aff. ति)
def: s.f. Measure
subs: s.f. Idem (and the more com. form).

def: s. f. Measure.

ety: S. मायि+फलं
def: s.m. Gall-nut (syn. mājū-phal, q.v.).

adj. corr. of موافق muwāfiq, q.v. adj. Conformable, consonant, congruous, agreeing, according, concordant, suiting, suitable; apt, expedient; like, similar, analogous; prosperous, favourable, propitious; — postpn. Conformably, or agreeably (to, -ke), &c.
subs: v.n, To agree (with, -ko), to suit; to prove favourable, or propitious (to)
adj. (f. -kī), Fitting, appropriate; adequate, sufficient; — middling; not much; passable
(-ke), To be in agreement, &c. (with), to agree (with); to consist (with); to be agreeable (to); to be like or similar (to), &c.

def: adj. & s.f. (dialec.; prob. for mātī) = mat-wālī, fem. of mat-wālā, q.v.

ety: māp˚ = Prk. माप्प(इ) = S. माप्य(ते), caus. pass. fr. rt. मा (used actively)+nā = अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To measure; to survey
more: v. a. To measure. ماپا شوربا اورگني ڐليان māpā shorbā aur ginī ḍaliyāṅ, Is applied to express scarcity of provision, also penurious economy.

ety: fr. the trans. māpnā
def: v.n. To be measured; — v.t. = māpnā, q.v. v.t. To measure; to survey
more: (intrans. of ماپنا) v. n. To be measured.

ety: doub. caus. of māpnā; and = māp˚+āw+āw, &c.; see mapānā
def: v.t. To cause to be measured, to have or get (a thing) measured.
more: (causal of ماپنا) v. a. To cause to measure.

ety: conj. part. of mārnā, and = Prk. मारिअ or मारि; S. मारयित्वा
def: part. Having beaten, &c. (see mārnā; — mār is frequently prefixed adverbially to other verbs, and intensifies or otherwise affects their signification, as will be seen from the following examples)
subs: v.t. To beat and drive away; to put to flight
v.t. To lay dead, to slay, kill; — to withhold (wages, &c.) wrongfully
v.n. To live by plunder or spoliation, to earn by robbery, to prey (upon)
v.t. To knock down; to complete the destruction of
(-ko), To take or obtain by plunder or robbery, to plunder, rob
v.n. 'To smite oneself and die,' to commit suicide; — to slay (some of the enemy) and die, to do and die (also mārke marnā)
v.t. To beat and drive back, to beat back; to overthrow.

ety: Pehl. mār; Zend māra, rt. mar; S. मार, rt. मर्
def: s.m. A snake, serpent
subs: s.f. Serpentine twisting and winding; circumvolution; crooked manœuvring, crafty device, intrigue, chicanery

ety: conj. part. of marnā; and = Prk. मरिअ = S. मृत्वा, rt. मृ
def: part. Having died, &c. (frequent as first member of verbal compounds, as mar-paćnā, &c., q.v.s.v. marnā).

ety: fr. mār-nā, q.v.
def: s.f. Beating, striking; — a beating, a blow, stroke; punishment; affliction; — battle; a firing (at), cannonading, bombarding; — a rushing upon, a crowding round; — plunder, extortion, exaction; — cure, remedy (e.g. khūjlī-kī mār ghī hai); — lure, temptation, snare; — a kind of stiff clay or loamy soil with sand and vegetable mould; a rich black soil
more: n. f. 1. Beating; striking. 1. Lohe kī manḍī meṅ mār hī mār . Prov. In the iron in art it's hammer hammer all. 2. Mār ke āge bhūt nāchtā hai . Prov. Before the whip devils dance. 2. A blow; stroke. 3. Wrath; famine. 1. Roṭī kī mār . 2. Tujh pe Bhagvān kī mār paṛe! A curse. 4. ( In Comp .) Killing; destroyer. 5. Lure; temptation; snare. 6. Cure; remedy. Khujlī kī mār ghī hai . 7. Stiff clay or loamy soil with some sand and vegetable mould; ( Bundelkhand ) a rich black loam. mār baiṭhnā , To strike; beat. mār bhagānā , v. a. To put to flight. mār paṛnā , v. n. To be beaten. Akhto ne pakāī bariyāṅ, Bakhto ne pakāī dāl, Akhto kī jal gaīṅ baṛiyāṅ, to Bakhto pe paṛī mār . The co-wife's song in a Puppet-show. Akhto cooked pulse balls, and Bakhto cooked some dāl , Burnt were Akhto's pulse balls, blows on Bakhto fall. mār-pīṭ, mār-piṭāī, mār-dhāṛ, mār-kuṭāī , n. f. Beating and bruising; fighting;…

s. f. (dakh. s. m.) 1. Beating. 2. Battle. 3. A blow. 4. In compos. Killing, killer. مارپاڙو mār-pāṛū, adj. (dakh.) Deadly. مارپڙنا mār paṛnā, To be beaten. مارپيٿ mār-pīṭ, s. f. Beating and bruising, drubbing. ماردهار mār-dhār, s. f. Thumping and beating severely. مار دينا mār denā, To smite, to beat. مار ڐالنا mār ḍālnā, To kill, to smite. مار سکنا mār saknā, To be able to beat. مار کٿاٴي mār-kuṭāʼī, s. f. Beating and bruising. مار کهاني mār-khānī, To get a drubbing. مار گرانا mār girānā, To knock down. مار لانا mār lānā, To rob, to take by robbery. مار لينا mār lenā, To smite, to overcome, to conquer. مار مرنا mār marnā, 1. To commit suicide. 2. To fall in battle after killing some of the enemy. مارهارنا mār hārnā, To beat severely. مار هٿانا mār haṭānā, To overcome, to beat and drive back. مار मार mār, s. m. A name of Kāmadeva. See کام kām.

s. m. A snake, a serpent. مارپيچ mār-pech, s. f. Circumvolution, serpentine twisting and winding. مارپيچ کي راه mār-pech kī rāh, A crooked or winding path. مار گير mār-gīr, s. m. A snake-catcher. مار گيري mār-gīr-ī, s. f. Snake-catching.
subs: (-par), A beating (or punishment) to fall (upon); to be beaten
mār-piṭāʼī, mār-dhār, or mār-kuṭāʼī, s.f. Beating and bruising; assault and battery; fight, affray, struggle
(-ko), To give a beating (to), to beat, to smite
adj. & s.m. Quarrelsome, turbulent; — a turbulent fellow, a brawler
v.n. To suffer a beating, to get a drubbing, to be beaten (by, -se)
(-ko), To get (another) a beating, to cause (one) to suffer punishment, to have (one) punished; to punish
To go in plunder or spoliation, &c., to be made away with.

ety: see mar-nā
def: s.m. Dying.

ety: mār, q.v. + Prk. अओ = S. अ+कः
def: s.m. A striking, beating; a blow, &c. ( = mār); an attack (as on a fort, &c.), bombarding, &c.; a line (of fire); range or reach (of fire); — a violent and general destruction; — overwhelming profusion or plenty (of anything)
more: adj. 1. Struck down; smitten. 1. Kāl kā mārā . Famine-struck. 2. Sardī kā mārā . Frost-bitten. 2. Killed; slain. mārā jānā , v. n. 1. To be killed, slain. Chāpke se mil mujh se, dugānā, tere vārī jāūṅ! Muft meṅ aisā na ho maiṅ kahīṅ mārī jāūṅ! Wom. 2. To be ruined, wrecked, undone. 3. To be overturned or sunk (a boat). 4. To be cut off (a caravan, detachment, etc.). 5. To be lost. 6. To go away; to be non est. Sāre naukar kahāṅ māre gaë? Are all the servants dead? mārā mār, mār , n. f. 1. Struggle; bustle. 2. Jostle; rush; crush. Kām kī mārā mār hai . 3. Scuffle; fray; broil. mārā mārā phirnā, mārā phirnā , v. n. 1. To wander about. 1. Ṭuk hirs o havā ko chhoṛ, miyāṅ! mat des ba-des phire mārā! Nazīr. 2. Aisī hotī kātan-hārī to kāhe ko phirtī mārī . Prov. Could you have spun so you would not have been knocked about so. 2. To be knocked about. mārā mār k. , v. a. 1. To work hard; try one'…

Rus. adj. See bodā , (1, 3). 1. Weak. Māṛā mānas . 2. A little. Arī kisāṅṛīṅ, māṛā sā sīt manne bhī de! Rus. O country woman, give me too a little buttermilk! 3. Poor. Māṛī sākh . 4. Incompetent. 5. Bad. Māṛā ghī nāṅ hai . 6. Trifling. Māṛīchīj . 7. Mean; low. Māṛī bāt nā kaheṇ saëṅ . 8. Close-fisted. Māṛā jī hai .

adj. 1. Beaten, smitten, slain. 2. Foundered, or overturned (a boat, &c.). 3. (subst.) A victim. مارا پڙنا mārā-paṛnā, To be killed or slain. مارا ماري mārā-mārī ( मारामारि ) 1. Mutual beating, scuffle, fray, bickering, broil. 2. Preposteræ veneris usus reciprocus. مارا مارا پهرنا mārā mārā phirnā, To wander.
subs: s.f. Beating and buffeting, &c. ( = mār-kuṭāʼī, q.v.s.v. mār)

or mārā-mārī, s.f. Mutual beating or striking; contention, struggle, scuffle, broil, fray; — jostling; bustling; rush, crush; varied and toilsome efforts (as after the means of subsistence, &c.); — exceeding plentifulness (of); — preposterœ venis usus reciprocus: mārā-mār karnā, To bustle vehemently about; to make all haste; to toil hard; to try one's best

ety: perf. part. of mārnā, and = Prk. मारिअओ = S. मारित+कः

more: n. m. 1. A male; a man. Mard kī sūrat ho-kar kamāyā nahīṅ jātā . In form and face a man, but earn nothing you can. 2. A husband. Mard kā dikhāyā na khāīye, mard kā lāyā khāīye! Wom. Prov. Eat not before your husband, eat what he brings you! 3. A fellow; chap; wight. Mujh se bāteṅ na ab ziyādah banā! Chal, cha/?/khe mardue! havās meṅ ā! Mah. Wom. Shauq. 4. A brave man; a hero. mard-bāz , Slang. adj. Lascivious, lustful (woman). mard-raugan , n. m. Semen. mard kī zāt . The male sex. mard kī sūrat na dekhnā , v. n. lit . not to see a man's face. Not to know a man; to live single (a woman).

Man-trying or -proving. مرد باز mard-bāz, A lascivious woman, adultress, strumpet. مرد بازي mard-bāz-ī, s. f. Adultery. مرد بچه mard-bachcha, The son of a brave man. (Pers. plur. مردان).

n. m. S. मृग, Pālī, mago . A deer; an antelope. Sāvan khīr jo khāë sakāre, mirag ḍhāl karchhālāṅ māre . Rus. In August who each morn rice-milk doth eat Will bound along as doth the antelope fleet. mirg-trishnā ; A. surāb , n. m. The mirage. mirg-chiṛā , n. m. 1. A small bird. 2. A small insect living under the river bank. mirg-chhālā , n. f. A deer skin worn and used as bedding by devotees by whom it is held sacred. mirg-rāj , n. m. lit . king of beasts. A lion. mirg-sālā , n. f. A park of deer. mirg-nābh , n. m. 1. The navel of a deer. 2. A bag of musk. mirg-nainī, mirgā-lochnī, mirag kī sī āṅkheṅ , Poet. adj. Fawn-eyed. 1. Mirg-nainī, haṅs-gamnī . 2. Mirg kī sī āṅkheṅ, chīte kī sī kamar .

s. m. A deer. مرگ ترشنا mirg-tr̤ish̤ṇā ( मृगतृष्णा ) s. m. See سراب sarāb. مرگ چڙا mirg-chiṛā, s. m. Name of a small bird. مرگ چهالا mirg-chhāl-ā, The skin of any antelope worn and used as a bed by devotees. مرگ سالا mirg-sālā, A deer-park. مرگ لوچني mirg-lochan-ī ( मृगलोचन ) Having an eye like the deer. مرگ مد mirg-mad ( मृगमद ) s. m. Musk. مرگ نابهہ mirg-nābhi ( मृगनाभि ) s. m. Musk, a bag of musk. مرگ نيني mirg-nain-ī, or mr̤ig-nayan-ī ( मृगनयन ) Deer-eyed. (An epithet of a mistress).

def: s.m. A vital part (of the body), vulnerable or mortal spot; tender place; weak organ, or part, or point (in); — any vital member or organ; the core (of anything); the quick; a joint; — anything which requires to be kept concealed, secret foible; secret design or purpose; hidden meaning; — anything hidden or recondite; secret recess (of the heart, &c.), the innermost heart; — a secret, mystery; truth
more: n. m. S. मर्म्म 1. Hidden meaning or purpose; a secret. 1. Bindrāban ke birachh ko maram na jāne koë, Ḍāl ḍāl aur pāt pāt meṅ Har kī charchā hoë . The heart of Bindraban's bright groves none know, Each branch and leaf with Hari's praises glow. 2. Rāg tāl ke maram na jāne, dono hāth majīrā! Prov. Nor song nor time he knows, cymbals both hands enclose! 2. Mind; heart. 1. Mātā tere rāj māṅ sukhī rahī din rāt, Ib koī īsā nahīṅ jo sune maram kī bāt . Rus. Wom. Mother! happy I was with you night and day, Now no one to listen to my heart's sad lay. 2. Dharā ḍhakā sab pūchhan lāge kuṭum, putr, pari-vārā, Darad, maram kī koī na pūchhe—matlab kā jag sārā . Bhajan. 'Where is your money hid? his relatives all keen to know, Nor dying wish nor groans they heed—all selfishness below.
s. m. Secret meaning or purpose, a secret, any thing hidden or recondite.
subs: or marma-vedī, adj. & s.m. (f. -ā, and inī), Knowing weak or vulnerable points; knowing secrets or the hidden circumstances (of any affair); knowing mysteries, familiar with the most secret or recondite portions of a subject, deeply versed (in); any very acute or learned man; a Brāhmaṇ; a spy
or maram-sthān, s.m. A vital part, vulnerable place; a tender spot, &c.

def: v.t. To beat, strike, thump, smite; to hit, knock; to punish; — to kill, slay, slaughter; to destroy, ruin, mar, spoil, blight, blast; to crack, break; to deaden or blunt (the edge of a knife, &c.); to destroy the active qualities of, to kill (quicksilver, lead, &c.), to calcine; — to strike out; to thrust out; — to attack (villages, houses, or travellers); to rob or seize with assault (i.e. with killing, or personal violence), to plun der; to embezzle; to withhold wrongfully; — to earn smartly and cleverly; to make (profit in business); — to stop, close; to fine; — to overcome, conquer, vanquish, defeat; to seize, take, capture, catch; to master subdue. tame, mortify (affections, appetites, or lusts); to calm, quench, allay, suppress, smother; to counteract (poison, &c.); — to press down; to stamp; — to drive; to drive or ram in; to insert; to apply, fix, fasten, clap on; — to set; to throw, to pelt with, to cast (a spell); to toss; to shoot, discharge, fire (a gun), dart or hurl (a missile), lay on (a sword, &c.); to fight (a battle); to strike or make (a sudden assault); to strike up (a run, dance, strain, &c.); to strike out (the hands or legs); to pitch (a tent); to chuck in, or toss off (a morsel); to raise or set up (a cry); to make or do (generally with vehemence, eagerness, or smartness); — (as a gerund) s.m. Striking, beating, &c.
more: v. a. See pīṭnā . 1. To beat; strike; thrash; knock down. 1. Mujh ko na māre to maiṅ sāre jahān ko mār āūṅ . ( Ironic .) Prov. Let no one strike me and I'll whip all creation. 2. Qadar-dān kī jūtīyāṅ uṭhāīye, nā-qadre ke pāposh mārne na jāīye . Prov. Hold the shoes of the just, deign not to shoe-beat the unjust. 2. To punish. 3. To drive away. Kutte ko māro! 4. To spurn; despise. Jūtī par mārtā hūṅ . 5. To kill; slay. Mārūṅ sūar, na jabhūṅ gāë, Kalmāṅ paṛhūṅ, na Rām kahāë . Kabīr. Nor pig I slay, nor slaughter e'er a cow, Nor read Koran, nor to Ram's name I bow. 6. To destroy; ruin. 7. To slay with kindness. Khūb rokā shikāëtoṅ se mujhe, Tū ne mārā ināëtoṅ se mujhe . Zauq. Complaints that I could make you've hushed right well, Your favours seal my lips as with a spell. 8. To keep down; bear up. Bhūk yā piyās mārnā . 2. Aise jogī phireṅ bahut se bhabūt-dhārā, jī, Nahīṅ karī īndrī bas, āpā…

( मारण or caus. of مرنا marnā, q. v.) v. a. To smite, strike, hit, kill, slay, beat, bang, drive, shoot, punish, mortify, cast, mar, blight, blast, spoil, ruin, conquer, crack, destroy, break, fine, take, set, smother, stamp, sting, stop, calm, throw, toss, quench, run. (subst.) Striking, &c.
subs: v.t. (intens.) To kill outright, to slay, to murder
v.t. (intens.) To beat completely, to overcome, conquer, &c.

ety: mar˚ = Prk. मर(इ) = S. म्रिय(ते), but Vedic also मर(ति), rt. मृ
def: v.n. To die, expire; to cease; to be dead (as in a game); to fade, wither; to be lost (as a stake, or a bad debt); to become bankrupt or insolvent (in these senses mar-jānā is the more com. form); — to die or be dying (for, -par), to set the heart (upon), to be desperately enamoured (of), or in love (with), to desire vehemently; — to labour or toil hard (for, -ko), to sweat and groan, to suffer hardship; — (as a gerund) s.m. Dying, death
more: v. n. 1. To die; expire. 1. Dhī marī, jaṅvāī chor; ṭūṭ gaī ḍālī, uṛ gayā mor . Prov. The daughter dead the son-in-law skulks stealthily alone, when breaks the branch the bird is flown. 2. Ādmī mualā par, chīj bhulailā par sūjhelā . Bhoj. Prov. When men and things have passed away their worth then to your heart you lay. 3. Kauṛī, kauṛī māyā joṛī, joṛ jamīṅ meṅ dhartā hai, Jiske lahne kī hai voh khāgā, pāpī bah bah martā hai . Bhajan. Farthings and pence heaped up, a store of wealth in earth hid lies, For him t' enjoy whose fate it is, the miser pines and dies. 4. Ann binā jaise pirān dukhī taise chhoṭe balam kī nār dukhārī, Oṛhat hūṅ ilṛī dulṛī, mohe lāgat ang aṅgār se bhārī, Aur abhākan ko pahne mohe pāyan mhāvaṛ lāgat bhārī, Aise jīvan se mar jāno bhalo, barsāt gaī piyā sang na soī! Kabit. As bread without life pines so aches the bosom of lone wife, The jewels which I wear worse than ho…
v. n. 1. To die, to expire, to cease. 2. ( met. ) To desire vehemently, to set oneʼs heart upon any thing, to dote. s. m. Dying.
subs: v.n. To work (oneself) to death, to labour excessively; to suffer pain or sorrow
v.n. To die; to fade, &c. (see marnā); to die away, die off; to be as though dead; to swoon
v.n. To recover from death; — s.m. Escape from the jaws of death
v.n. To die; to be dead; to lie dead; — to be dying (for, -par), &c., see marnā
v.n. To die and be effaced; to be killed or slain; — to be ruined, be sacrificed (for, -ke pīćhe)
or marne-hārā, adj. (f. -ī), & s.m. Doomed to die, dying, perishable, mortal; — a mortal
adj. Fit or deserving to die
About to die, at the point of death
To have no time to die; to be overwhelmed with work, to have too much to do to think of dying
To die prematurely; to die a violent death.

more: (corrupt. of مسن misann, noun of instrument from سنّ Heb. ) s. f. A small whetstone.

def: s.m. = marsā, q.v. s.m. A species of pot-herb, Amaranthus oleraceus (syn. māṭ or māṭh).
more: n. m. S. मारिष A plant yielding a small edible grain largely consumed in the hills; amaranthus oleraceus , Wat.

ety: for A. مرثية mars̤iyat, inf. n. of رثي 'to weep over, to bewail'
def: s.m. A lamentation for one dead, an elegy, a dirge, an epicedeum (esp. the one sung during the Moḥarram in commemoration of Hasan and Hosain)
more: n. m. رثي bewailed. The dirge of Hasan and Hosain recited at the Moharram ; an elegy.

(inf. of رثي) s. m. An elegy, a dirge, a funeral eulogium, particularly one sung during the muḥarram in commemoration of the descendants of Aﻌlī. مرثيه خوان mars̤iya-ḵẖẉān, A repeater of an elegy or of the mars̤iya. مرثيه خواني mars̤iya-ḵẖẉān-ī, Repeating, or singing an elegy or the mars̤iya.
subs: s.m. Reciter or chanter of a mars̤iya
s.f. Reciting or chanting of a mars̤iya.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَرْثِیَه (marsiya), from Arabic مَرْثِيَة (marṯiya).

def: n. m. A reader of the marsiyā , q. v. marsiyah-/?/khuānī , n. f. Chanting of the marsīyah .

ety: pass. part. of رسم 'to mark, to stamp,' &c.
def: part. Signed, marked, notified, sketched out, described; prescribed; — accustomed.
more: (part. pass. of رسم) adj. Accustomed, prescribed.

ety: imperf. part. of mārnā; and = Prk. मारंतओ; S. मारत् (अन्त+कः)
def: part. Beating, striking, &c. (see mārnā); — s.m. One who strikes or beats
subs: s.m. The one who can overcome, or cure, or teach a lesson (to), &c.; the match (for), the masterer (of), the man (or boy, or lad, for)
s.m. One who is addicted to striking, &c.; a tyrant, an oppressor.

ety: imperf. part. of marnā, q.v.
def: adj. (f. -ī), Dying; — s.m. A dying man
subs: s.m. The time of death, the dying moments.

r: martaba, martabat, rutba, is martabe-tak, ek martaba, kamāl martaba
ety: v.n. fr. رتب 'to be or become firm, or steady,' &c.
def: adv. To (or in) perfection, perfectly, completely
more: n. m. 1. Rank; office ( padvī , 1). 2. Time. kaī martabah . Several times; repeatedly. kaī martabah aisā huā . Several instances have occurred.
s. m. (from رتب) A step, degree, dignity, office, employment, charge, rank of honour, class, order, time, turn. اس مرتبه is martaba, To this degree.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مرتبه (martaba), from Arabic مَرْتَبَة (martaba, “status, degree, place, division”).

def: s.m. A glazed (earthenware) jar (used for keeping preserves and pickles, &c. in); a vessel of the finest porcelain which poison cannot penetrate (also written mart̤abān; corrupt forms are maratbān and imratbān).
more: n. m. Cor. of martūb-bān . A glazed jar.
s. m. A jar for keeping preserves in, a vessel of the finest China porcelain which poison cannot penetrate.

def: s.m. = مرتبان martabān, q.v. s.m. A glazed (earthenware) jar (used for keeping preserves and pickles, &c. in); a vessel of the finest porcelain which poison cannot penetrate (also written mart̤abān; corrupt forms are maratbān and imratbān).

r: martabat, martaba, rutba, is martabe-tak, ek martaba, kamāl martaba
ety: v.n. fr. رتب 'to be or become firm, or steady,' &c.
def: adv. To (or in) perfection, perfectly, completely
more: s. f. (from رتب) A step, degree, dignity, office, employment, charge, rank of honour, class, order, time, turn. اس مرتبه is martaba, To this degree.

ety: S. मृतक + इल+इकः
def: adj. (dialec.) = mariyal, q.v. adj. & s.m. Half-dead, having no life (in him, or it, &c.), more dead than alive, moribund; — lean, emaciated, much reduced (by sickness, &c.); weak, feeble; — lethargic, indolent, lazy, slow, inactive; — a half-dead-and-alive person; a lean or emaciated person, &c.

ety: S. मृतक + इल+इकः
def: adj. (dialec.) = mariyal, q.v. adj. & s.m. Half-dead, having no life (in him, or it, &c.), more dead than alive, moribund; — lean, emaciated, much reduced (by sickness, &c.); weak, feeble; — lethargic, indolent, lazy, slow, inactive; — a half-dead-and-alive person; a lean or emaciated person, &c.
more: adj. 1. Lean; emaciated. 2. Weak; feeble; moribund. 3. Lazy; indolent.

def: s.m. corr. fr. markat, q.v.

adj. (dialec.) = martailiyā, and mariyal, qq.v. adj. & s.m. Half-dead, having no life (in him, or it, &c.), more dead than alive, moribund; — lean, emaciated, much reduced (by sickness, &c.); weak, feeble; — lethargic, indolent, lazy, slow, inactive; — a half-dead-and-alive person; a lean or emaciated person, &c.
s.m. The sun; — a hog.
adj. (f. -ā), Doomed to die, liable to death, mortal.
The world of mortals, the earth. See under لوک lok.
s.m. corr. of martya-lok, q.v.

ety: Portug. martello
def: s.m. A hammer; — a turnscrew.
wety: Borrowed from Portuguese martelo.
